title: Trading and Exchanges
subtitle: Market Microstructure for Practitioners
author: Larry Harris
authorBio: Larry Harris is the Fred V. Keenan Chair in Finance at the University of Southern California Marshall School of Business. He served as Chief Economist of the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (2002-2004). His research focuses on market microstructure, trading rules, and regulation. He is widely regarded as one of the world's leading experts on market structure.
notableQuotes: Most traders lose money — and the people who know this are the ones who make it.
Liquidity is the ability to trade when you want at a low cost.
A market is a place where people who want to trade can find each other.
The zero-sum game of trading is actually worse than zero-sum because of transaction costs.
